The Cambodia Edge — Garment Economy + EBA Dependency + Dollarization
🏭 Cambodia: The EBA-Dependent Garment Canary

Cambodia has built its modern economy almost entirely on a single structural pillar: garment and textile exports (~38% of GDP) accessing EU and US markets under preferential trade terms. This creates a uniquely fragile but currently stable intelligence signal.


Three structural features make Cambodia distinct:

  • EBA Dependency: The EU's Everything But Arms (EBA) scheme grants Cambodia zero-tariff access for all exports except arms. With EU absorbing ~45% of garment exports ($4.8B), EBA suspension is the single highest-impact policy risk. A 2020 partial EBA suspension (over labor rights) already demonstrated the transmission mechanism.
  • Full Dollarization: Cambodia uses USD as its primary currency. This eliminates FX volatility risk but removes the NBC's ability to use monetary policy to absorb shocks. Any liquidity stress is immediate and structural.
  • Chinese Capital Dependency: Chinese FDI (especially Sihanoukville) peaked 2019-2020 and collapsed sharply. The Sihanoukville implosion (Chinese diaspora exodus 2021-2022) serves as a live case study: when Chinese capital pulled back, local property and service sectors collapsed 70%+ before stabilizing.

KC_02 Garment (weight 0.30) is the highest-weighted kill condition in NBC_CAMBODIA_CANARY. EBA suspension is the primary tail risk: a full EBA suspension would instantly push KC_02 from 0.25 NORMAL to 0.90 CRITICAL and CKRI to WATCH+ regardless of other conditions. This is Cambodia's structural single-point-of-failure.

EBA: Cambodia's Tariff Shield
EU EBA grants zero-tariff access to ~$4.8B in annual garment exports. Without EBA, Cambodia would face standard EU garment tariffs of 12-17%, making it uncompetitive vs Bangladesh, Vietnam, and other peers. EBA compliance (labor rights, governance) is therefore a continuous policy risk requiring monitoring.
Garment = 38% GDP
No other country in ASEAN has such concentrated GDP exposure to a single export sector under a single preferential trade scheme. Cambodia's garment workers (~700,000 employed) represent the country's largest formal employment sector. A garment order collapse directly hits household income, consumption, and banking stability simultaneously.
PVH & HBI: Most Exposed US Stocks
PVH Corp (Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger) and Hanesbrands source 11% and 13% of their product respectively from Cambodia. These are the highest US stock Cambodia sourcing concentrations. EBA suspension or a garment order collapse = direct input cost shock for both companies, with no short-term sourcing alternative at scale.

KC_02 HIGHEST WEIGHT (0.30): EBA suspension → KC_02 score immediately to 0.90 CRITICAL → CKRI pushed above 0.30 WATCH regardless of all other kill conditions. Garment export order collapse is Cambodia's structural single-point-of-failure.

PVH + HBI Dual Garment Concentration: Of all US-listed apparel companies, PVH Corp and Hanesbrands have the highest proportional Cambodia sourcing (11% and 13% respectively). An EBA suspension or major garment order collapse hits both companies simultaneously with no short-term alternative sourcing at the scale required. KKR is the primary non-garment exposure via Chinese FDI portfolio risk in Cambodia real estate and infrastructure.
Garment Sector Monitor — KC_02 (Score: 0.25 NORMAL) | Highest Weight: 0.30
Primary Risk Vector Alert Framework: KC_02 Garment (weight 0.30) is the highest-weighted kill condition in NBC_CAMBODIA_CANARY. EBA suspension is the key tail event: it would instantly push KC_02 from current score 0.25 NORMAL to 0.90 CRITICAL, driving CKRI above 0.30 WATCH. The 2020 partial EBA suspension precedent (over labor rights in 2020) demonstrated this transmission mechanism directly. Monitor EU EBA compliance reviews, labor rights NGO reports, and EU GSP committee announcements continuously.

Total Cambodia garment exports 2025: ~$10.6B. Employment: ~700,000 workers directly employed. EBA value to Cambodia: ~$5.5B annually in trade preference savings at standard EU tariff rates. A full EBA suspension would eliminate the price advantage and redirect EU buyers to Bangladesh, Vietnam, and Myanmar within 12-24 months.

EBA Risk Monitor — The Primary Tail Risk
Risk FactorLevelDetailStatus
EU EBA Status ACTIVE Everything But Arms scheme fully in force as of Q3 2026. Cambodia retained market access after 2020 partial review. 🟢 ACTIVE — Monitor
Labor Rights Compliance ONGOING WATCH EU monitors freedom of association, labor rights, and political freedoms. 2020 suspension precedent over Kem Sokha arrest and opposition crackdown. Compliance reviews periodic. 🟡 WATCH — Structural Risk
EBA Suspension Impact -$4.2B Full EBA suspension would eliminate ~$4.2B in annual trade preference value. KC_02 score: 0.25 → 0.90. CKRI: 0.1860 → 0.45+ WATCH immediately. 🔴 TAIL RISK
2020 Precedent DEMONSTRATED EU suspended ~20% of EBA preferences in 2020 over political crackdown. Cambodia granted concessions to restore. Full suspension remains the primary tail scenario. 🟡 Precedent Active
Bangladesh / Vietnam Competition STRUCTURAL EBA suspension would accelerate EU buyer shift to Bangladesh (also EBA-eligible) and Vietnam (EVFTA zero-tariff). Cambodia has no alternative trade anchor at scale. 🟡 Diversion Risk
Chinese FDI Monitor — KC_03 (Score: 0.20 NORMAL)
IndicatorReadingDetailStatus
Chinese FDI (2026 est.) ~$1.9B Declining from $3.1B peak in 2019. Manufacturing FDI (BRI-linked) stable; Sihanoukville real estate and casino FDI collapsed. 🟢 STABLE — Declining Trend
Sihanoukville Index 35 (2019=100) Chinese presence index crashed from 120 (peak 2020) to 25 (trough 2023). Slow recovery to 35 by 2026. Chinese casino and real estate bubble fully deflated. 🟡 Recovering — Slow
BRI Manufacturing Flows STABLE BRI-linked manufacturing (textiles, electronics assembly) continues despite Sihanoukville collapse. China-Cambodia free trade area expanding bilateral trade. 🟢 NORMAL
Acute Pullback Risk LOW No signals of accelerated Chinese capital exit Q3 2026. Hun Manet government maintaining Beijing relations. ASEAN neutrality intact. 🟢 NO ACUTE RISK

The Sihanoukville implosion (2021-2022) was the most significant Chinese capital event in Cambodia's recent history: Chinese diaspora presence collapsed from ~250,000 to ~30,000 as Cambodia cracked down on online scam operations and Chinese casino licensing. Property prices fell 70%+. The recovery is slow and incomplete — this serves as the live stress-test for KC_03 acute pullback scenarios.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2100  NORMAL

National Bank of Cambodia operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (significant dependency, weight 0.40).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, industrial machinery, consumer electronics, food commodities. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Cambodia is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — garments, textiles, footwear, rice — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2100 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Strait of Malacca closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  FDI slowdown or political reversal on nearshoring → deferred logistics investment  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2000  NORMAL

National Bank of Cambodia is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Cambodia real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Cambodia, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2000 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2800  WATCH

National Bank of Cambodia faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Flood, Drought Heatwave.

The insurance market in Cambodia is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2800 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Flood event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
